Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "NCCOB" or "examination" or "regulator" (or similar) using search_emails (query="NCCOB" or similar, folder_name="INBOX"), or browse the inbox using list_emails (folder_name="INBOX"), to find the unread email from examinations@nccob.gov (email_id: email_inject_ncob_exam_notice_001) with subject "Notice of Targeted Financial Controls Examination - Keystone Mortgage Partners (NMLS #1784320)". The email details a targeted examination under N.C.G.S. 53-244.114 covering: (1) vendor payment authorization and approval workflows, (2) commission and compensation payment controls for active and separated staff, (3) fee reconciliation between borrower-facing charges and vendor payments. The notice explicitly states the deadline as May 12, 2026. CC'd to robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com. is_read=false.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "examination" or "NCCOB" (or similar) using search_emails (query="examination" or similar, folder_name="SENT"), or browse sent mail using list_emails (folder_name="SENT"), to find Denise's forwarded message to Grace (email_id: email_inject_denise_grace_exam_fwd_002) in SENT folder from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, CC robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com. This email connects the NCCOB examination scope to the Premier Data Solutions situation and the Marcus Webb commission question, and requests (1) full scope of PDS payments from Hana, (2) complete list of post-departure commission payments to Marcus Webb, (3) current vendor approval workflow details.
OE 3: Search emails with a query related to "Premier Data Solutions" or "Hana" (or similar) using search_emails (query="Premier Data Solutions" or similar), or browse the inbox using list_emails, to find Hana Kim's update about Premier Data Solutions payment history (email_id: email_inject_hana_premier_update_003) from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. Hana reports: Victor originally found bills going back to August 2025, but she found two more miscoded under Office Supplies (October and November 2025). Actual total: 10 months x $600 = $6,000 (not $4,800 originally reported). All approved through single-signature workflow (under $1,000 threshold). Payment routing goes through an unfamiliar payment processor. is_read=false.
OE 4: Search emails with a query related to "forensic" or "Premier Data" or "preliminary findings" (or similar) using search_emails to find the forensic accountant's preliminary findings (email_id: email_inject_melissa_forensic_004) from mhart@hartforensicaccounting.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. The forensic accountant reports: (1) Premier Data Solutions LLC registered with NC Secretary of State on July 14, 2025, two weeks before first invoice; registered address is a UPS Store mailbox at 4915 Nations Crossing Rd, Suite 412, Charlotte, NC 28217; (2) all ten invoices follow identical $600 pattern with no variance, no documentation, no contract; (3) single-signature approval gap for amounts under $1,000; (4) also flags South Park Insurance Group: vendor marked inactive in QuickBooks but an April 2026 bill posted for $702.86, and questions who is approving bills for a vendor the company deactivated. is_read=false.
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "Robert" or "examination" or "exposure" (or similar) using search_emails to find Robert Calloway's response about the examination (email_id: email_inject_robert_exam_response_005) from robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. Robert notes the anonymous tip means someone inside or close to Keystone went to the state. He requests answers on: total PDS payments, total commission payments to Marcus Webb after departure, whether other departed staff are still receiving payments, and fee reconciliation gaps. is_read=false.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for Premier Data Solutions bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria for VendorRef containing "Premier Data Solutions" or vendor ID "143") or quickbooks_search_vendors (to find vendor ID first). Discover vendor "Premier Data Solutions" with Id "143" and 10 bills total at $600 each: BILL-INJ-PDS-0725 (July 2025, coded to "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0825 (August 2025, "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0925 (September 2025, "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-1025 (October 2025, "Office Supplies", miscoded), BILL-INJ-PDS-1125 (November 2025, "Office Supplies", miscoded), BILL-INJ-PDS-1225 (December 2025, "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0126 (January 2026, "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0226 (February 2026, "Data Services"), B-2108 (March 2026, DocNumber PDS-030126, "Monthly data services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0426 (April 2026, "Data Services"). Total: $6,000. Agent must search broadly because expense coding is inconsistent (two bills coded to "Office Supplies" instead of "Data Services"), confirming the prompt's warning that "the first search may not give you the full picture."
OE 7: Search Slack with a query related to "Premier Data" or "$600" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, or retrieve DM history using channels_list (channel_types="im") then conversations_history (channel_id="D_hana_victor") to find Hana and Victor's DM about Premier Data. Hana (keystone_d5f0a921b139) reports finding two more months of $600 bills miscoded under office supplies, total actually $6,000 not $4,800, and that she told Denise. Victor (keystone_01bffce7ee0b) replies noting the gap in expense reconciliation, $6,000 over 10 months with zero documentation and a UPS mailbox address, asks if payment routing matches existing vendor accounts, and flags that Marcus Webb is still showing commission transfers in Stripe even though he left in March. He flagged it to Grace weeks ago but does not think anyone stopped the payments.
OE 8: Search Stripe for Marcus Webb commission transfers using stripe_list_transfers (destination_account_id="acct_scenario_644_departed") with pagination to find Webb-related transfers. Discover 5 transfers to destination account "acct_scenario_644_departed", all described as "LO commission -- Webb": (1) tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn: $1,250.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Chen (LN-2026-00525)", (2) tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8: $1,875.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Patel (LN-2026-00527)", (3) tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4: $980.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Torres (LN-2026-00008)", (4) tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs: $1,450.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Kim (LN-2026-00211)" (metadata: auto_generated), (5) tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA: $2,100.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Okafor (LN-2026-00608)" (metadata: auto_generated). Total: $7,655.00. Two transfers were auto-generated by the system.
OE 9: Look up Marcus Webb's Stripe connected account details using stripe_get_connected_account (account_id="acct_scenario_644_departed") or stripe_list_connected_accounts. Discover the connected account "acct_scenario_644_departed" with business_name "Marcus Webb Consulting", email "marcus.webb.lo@gmail.com", type "express", metadata showing staff_id "los_staff_a583f044387a" and departed "2026-03-15". Charges and payouts are both still enabled. The payment channel was never shut down.
OE 10: Check Marcus Webb's status in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_list_staff (active_only=false) or mortgage_los_get_staff (staff_id="los_staff_a583f044387a") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query="Webb"). Discover Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a) is still flagged as is_active=true with no termination_date, even though he departed on March 15, 2026. This is the root cause of the auto-generated commission transfers, as the system still treats him as an active loan officer. His role is "loan_officer", specialization "Conventional, FHA", current_pipeline_count=5.
OE 11: Search CRM for engagement notes about how Marcus Webb's departure was handled using crm_list_engagements (optionally filtered by contact_ids after first finding Webb's contact ID via crm_search_contacts with full_name="Marcus Webb"), or by browsing engagements via crm_list_engagements or crm_show_data. Discover engagement note crm_eng_inject_grace_webb_offboard_001 titled "Webb offboarding gap - commission payments continued post-departure." This note documents that Raj Anand revoked LOS system access on March 17, 2026, but stated he was "unsure" about Stripe payment rails and assumed Hana would handle the financial side. Hana confirmed she did not receive any offboarding checklist or notification. Five commission transfers totaling $7,655 were sent to "Marcus Webb Consulting" (personal Gmail) since departure, with two flagged as auto_generated. The LOS staff table still shows Marcus as is_active=true with no termination_date.
OE 12: Look up the loan files tied to Marcus Webb's commission transfers using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00525"), mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00527"), mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00008"), mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00211"), mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00608"). Retrieve the loan details for each of the 5 loan files referenced in the Webb commission transfers. All 5 loans exist in the LOS. Discover the loan IDs, statuses, borrower information, and assigned loan officers for each. The current assigned_lo on these loans is not Marcus Webb (loans were reassigned after his departure), confirming that commission payments were misrouted to a departed LO who is no longer assigned to these files. This provides the audit trail needed for the examiner and identifies which specific loan files need activity notes.
OE 13: Search QuickBooks for inactive vendor with new bills using quickbooks_search_vendors (criteria for "South Park Insurance" or "inactive") and quickbooks_search_bills (criteria for vendor "V-SOUTHPARK-INS"). Discover vendor "South Park Insurance Group" (Id: V-SOUTHPARK-INS) with Active=false. Despite being inactive, there is an April 2026 bill (BILL-INJ-SPINS-0415) for $702.86 with description "E&O insurance renewal - April 2026 monthly premium" (TxnDate 2026-04-15, DueDate 2026-04-29, DocNumber REC-SPI-041526, Balance $702.86 unpaid, coded to "Insurance" account). The vendor continues to accumulate bills even though it was deactivated. Additionally, regular monthly E&O and general liability bills from South Park exist throughout 2024-2026 in the base data.
OE 14: Search Slack with a query related to "Marcus Webb" or "commission" (or similar) using conversations_history (channel_id="D_denise_grace") or conversations_search_messages to find the Grace-Denise DM about Marcus Webb. Grace (keystone_e304643b171b) details that Raj revoked LOS access on March 17 but Stripe payment rail was not in his offboarding scope; Hana never got an offboarding notification; nobody stopped commission transfers. Marcus has a personal account "Marcus Webb Consulting" using personal Gmail. Five transfers totaling over $7,600, two auto-generated because LOS still shows active. Denise (keystone_a989261d4d33) replies that the LOS staff record is the root cause. Marcus is flagged active with no termination date, feeding automated commission workflow. Need to fix immediately AND document why it wasn't caught. Between this, Premier Data, and inactive vendor billing, it's a pattern of control failures, not isolated incidents.
OE 15: Search Slack for Denise's message in the compliance-alerts channel using conversations_history (channel_id="C004"). Discover Denise's message (keystone_a989261d4d33) in the compliance-alerts channel (C004): "Heads up to leadership only -- we have received a regulatory examination notice from NCCOB. Scope covers vendor payment controls, separated-staff commission reconciliation, and fee audit. Deadline May 12. Do not discuss outside of Grace, Robert, Hana, and Victor. I will be scheduling individual working sessions this week. Preserve all financial records, vendor files, and payment authorization documentation."
OE 16: Send a detailed compliance findings email to Denise using send_email (sender="denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com", recipients=["denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"], subject=compliance findings subject, content=detailed findings covering all exposure areas with exact figures, root causes, and corrective actions). Expected content: comprehensive compliance findings email covering Premier Data Solutions (10 bills x $600 = $6,000 total, no contract, UPS mailbox address, entity registered July 14, 2025, inconsistent expense coding, single-signature approval gap), Marcus Webb commission leakage (5 transfers totaling $7,655 to acct_scenario_644_departed, LOS still shows active with no termination date, no one shut down Stripe payment rail, 2 auto-generated transfers), South Park Insurance Group (inactive vendor still accumulating bills, April 2026 bill for $702.86 posted despite deactivation), root causes and corrective actions for each area, and May 12 deadline reference.
OE 17: Send a separate email to Grace and Robert with key findings and deadline using send_email (sender="denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com", recipients=["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", "r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com"] or similar, cc=["denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"], subject=key findings subject, content=key findings summary with May 12 deadline). Expected content: email summarizing the key findings from the NCCOB examination response, including Premier Data vendor fraud risk, Marcus Webb commission leakage, and inactive vendor billing gap, and emphasizing the May 12 documentation deadline. Denise is CC'd. Alternative valid recipient email for Robert: robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (used in email threads throughout the universe).
OE 18: Add activity notes to loan files tied to the commission issue using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id=loan_id, action="note" or similar, detail=compliance note about commission investigation). Agent adds activity notes to the loan files associated with the 5 Marcus Webb commission transfers. The loan IDs correspond to loan numbers LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00608. Agent needs to first look up the internal loan_id for each loan number (e.g., using mortgage_los_get_loan or mortgage_los_search_loans), then call mortgage_los_add_activity for each. The notes should document the compliance investigation and the commission payment issue so the audit trail is present if the examiner pulls individual files. Agent may add notes to all 5 or a reasonable subset.
OE 19: Create a CRM engagement note capturing the examination response scope using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type="NOTE", body=full scope of examination response, title=examination response title). Expected content: a CRM engagement note that serves as the central record of the NCCOB examination response, covering the full scope: vendor payment controls (Premier Data Solutions), commission reconciliation (Marcus Webb), fee audit, inactive vendor billing (South Park Insurance Group), and the corrective action plan. Should reference the May 12 deadline.
OE 20: Post a message in the compliance-alerts channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id="C004", payload=general compliance response message). Expected content: a message in the compliance-alerts channel (C004) informing the core team that Keystone is in examination response mode. The message should be specific enough that the team knows to prioritize compliance work but general enough that company-wide specifics are not exposed, consistent with Denise's instruction to "keep it general enough that the rest of the company does not learn the specifics."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

The email details a targeted examination under N.C.G.S. 53-244.114 covering: (1) vendor payment authorization and approval workflows, (2) commission and compensation payment controls for active and separated staff, (3) fee reconciliation between borrower-facing charges and vendor payments. The notice explicitly states the deadline as May 12, 2026. CC'd to robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com. is_read=false.

Discover vendor "Premier Data Solutions" with Id "143" and 10 bills total at $600 each: BILL-INJ-PDS-0725 (July 2025, coded to "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0825 (August 2025, "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0925 (September 2025, "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-1025 (October 2025, "Office Supplies", miscoded), BILL-INJ-PDS-1125 (November 2025, "Office Supplies", miscoded), BILL-INJ-PDS-1225 (December 2025, "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0126 (January 2026, "Data Services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0226 (February 2026, "Data Services"), B-2108 (March 2026, DocNumber PDS-030126, "Monthly data services"), BILL-INJ-PDS-0426 (April 2026, "Data Services"). Agent must search broadly because expense coding is inconsistent (two bills coded to "Office Supplies" instead of "Data Services"), confirming the prompt's warning that "the first search may not give you the full picture."
OE 7: Search Slack with a query related to "Premier Data" or "$600" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, or retrieve DM history using channels_list (channel_types="im") then conversations_history (channel_id="D_hana_victor") to find Hana and Victor's DM about Premier Data. Hana (keystone_d5f0a921b139) reports finding two more months of $600 bills miscoded under office supplies, total actually $6,000 not $4,800, and that she told Denise. Victor (keystone_01bffce7ee0b) replies noting the gap in expense reconciliation, $6,000 over 10 months with zero documentation and a UPS mailbox address, asks if payment routing matches existing vendor accounts, and flags that Marcus Webb is still showing commission transfers in Stripe even though he left in March. He flagged it to Grace weeks ago but does not think anyone stopped the payments.
OE 8: Search Stripe for Marcus Webb commission transfers using stripe_list_transfers (destination_account_id="acct_scenario_644_departed") with pagination to find Webb-related transfers. Discover 5 transfers to destination account "acct_scenario_644_departed", all described as "LO commission -- Webb": (1) tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn: $1,250.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Chen (LN-2026-00525)", (2) tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8: $1,875.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Patel (LN-2026-00527)", (3) tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4: $980.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Torres (LN-2026-00008)", (4) tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs: $1,450.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Kim (LN-2026-00211)" (metadata: auto_generated), (5) tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA: $2,100.00, "LO commission -- Webb -- Okafor (LN-2026-00608)" (metadata: auto_generated). Total: $7,655.00. Two transfers were auto-generated by the system.
OE 9: Look up Marcus Webb's Stripe connected account details using stripe_get_connected_account (account_id="acct_scenario_644_departed") or stripe_list_connected_accounts. Discover the connected account "acct_scenario_644_departed" with business_name "Marcus Webb Consulting", email "marcus.webb.lo@gmail.com", type "express", metadata showing staff_id "los_staff_a583f044387a" and departed "2026-03-15". Charges and payouts are both still enabled. The payment channel was never shut down.
OE 10: Check Marcus Webb's status in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_list_staff (active_only=false) or mortgage_los_get_staff (staff_id="los_staff_a583f044387a") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query="Webb"). Discover Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a) is still flagged as is_active=true with no termination_date, even though he departed on March 15, 2026. This is the root cause of the auto-generated commission transfers, as the system still treats him as an active loan officer. His role is "loan_officer", specialization "Conventional, FHA", current_pipeline_count=5.
OE 11: Search CRM for engagement notes about how Marcus Webb's departure was handled using crm_list_engagements (optionally filtered by contact_ids after first finding Webb's contact ID via crm_search_contacts with full_name="Marcus Webb"), or by browsing engagements via crm_list_engagements or crm_show_data. Discover engagement note crm_eng_inject_grace_webb_offboard_001 titled "Webb offboarding gap - commission payments continued post-departure." This note documents that Raj Anand revoked LOS system access on March 17, 2026, but stated he was "unsure" about Stripe payment rails and assumed Hana would handle the financial side. Hana confirmed she did not receive any offboarding checklist or notification. Five commission transfers totaling $7,655 were sent to "Marcus Webb Consulting" (personal Gmail) since departure, with two flagged as auto_generated. The LOS staff table still shows Marcus as is_active=true with no termination_date.
OE 12: Look up the loan files tied to Marcus Webb's commission transfers using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00525"), mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00527"), mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00008"), mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00211"), mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number="LN-2026-00608"). Retrieve the loan details for each of the 5 loan files referenced in the Webb commission transfers. All 5 loans exist in the LOS. Discover the loan IDs, statuses, borrower information, and assigned loan officers for each. The current assigned_lo on these loans is not Marcus Webb (loans were reassigned after his departure), confirming that commission payments were misrouted to a departed LO who is no longer assigned to these files. This provides the audit trail needed for the examiner and identifies which specific loan files need activity notes.
OE 13: Search QuickBooks for inactive vendor with new bills using quickbooks_search_vendors (criteria for "South Park Insurance" or "inactive") and quickbooks_search_bills (criteria for vendor "V-SOUTHPARK-INS"). Discover vendor "South Park Insurance Group" (Id: V-SOUTHPARK-INS) with Active=false. Despite being inactive, there is an April 2026 bill (BILL-INJ-SPINS-0415) for $702.86 with description "E&O insurance renewal - April 2026 monthly premium" (TxnDate 2026-04-15, DueDate 2026-04-29, DocNumber REC-SPI-041526, Balance $702.86 unpaid, coded to "Insurance" account). The vendor continues to accumulate bills even though it was deactivated. Additionally, regular monthly E&O and general liability bills from South Park exist throughout 2024-2026 in the base data.
OE 14: Search Slack with a query related to "Marcus Webb" or "commission" (or similar) using conversations_history (channel_id="D_denise_grace") or conversations_search_messages to find the Grace-Denise DM about Marcus Webb. Grace (keystone_e304643b171b) details that Raj revoked LOS access on March 17 but Stripe payment rail was not in his offboarding scope; Hana never got an offboarding notification; nobody stopped commission transfers. Marcus has a personal account "Marcus Webb Consulting" using personal Gmail. Five transfers totaling over $7,600, two auto-generated because LOS still shows active. Denise (keystone_a989261d4d33) replies that the LOS staff record is the root cause. Marcus is flagged active with no termination date, feeding automated commission workflow. Need to fix immediately AND document why it wasn't caught. Between this, Premier Data, and inactive vendor billing, it's a pattern of control failures, not isolated incidents.
OE 15: Search Slack for Denise's message in the compliance-alerts channel using conversations_history (channel_id="C004"). Discover Denise's message (keystone_a989261d4d33) in the compliance-alerts channel (C004): "Heads up to leadership only -- we have received a regulatory examination notice from NCCOB. Scope covers vendor payment controls, separated-staff commission reconciliation, and fee audit. Deadline May 12. Do not discuss outside of Grace, Robert, Hana, and Victor. I will be scheduling individual working sessions this week. Preserve all financial records, vendor files, and payment authorization documentation."
OE 16: Send a detailed compliance findings email to Denise using send_email (sender="denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com", recipients=["denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"], subject=compliance findings subject, content=detailed findings covering all exposure areas with exact figures, root causes, and corrective actions). Expected content: comprehensive compliance findings email covering Premier Data Solutions (10 bills x $600 = $6,000 total, no contract, UPS mailbox address, entity registered July 14, 2025, inconsistent expense coding, single-signature approval gap), Marcus Webb commission leakage (5 transfers totaling $7,655 to acct_scenario_644_departed, LOS still shows active with no termination date, no one shut down Stripe payment rail, 2 auto-generated transfers), South Park Insurance Group (inactive vendor still accumulating bills, April 2026 bill for $702.86 posted despite deactivation), root causes and corrective actions for each area, and May 12 deadline reference.
OE 17: Send a separate email to Grace and Robert with key findings and deadline using send_email (sender="denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com", recipients=["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", "r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com"] or similar, cc=["denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"], subject=key findings subject, content=key findings summary with May 12 deadline). Expected content: email summarizing the key findings from the NCCOB examination response, including Premier Data vendor fraud risk, Marcus Webb commission leakage, and inactive vendor billing gap, and emphasizing the May 12 documentation deadline. Denise is CC'd. Alternative valid recipient email for Robert: robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (used in email threads throughout the universe).
OE 18: Add activity notes to loan files tied to the commission issue using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id=loan_id, action="note" or similar, detail=compliance note about commission investigation). Agent adds activity notes to the loan files associated with the 5 Marcus Webb commission transfers. The loan IDs correspond to loan numbers LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00608. Agent needs to first look up the internal loan_id for each loan number (e.g., using mortgage_los_get_loan or mortgage_los_search_loans), then call mortgage_los_add_activity for each. The notes should document the compliance investigation and the commission payment issue so the audit trail is present if the examiner pulls individual files. Agent may add notes to all 5 or a reasonable subset.
OE 19: Create a CRM engagement note capturing the examination response scope using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type="NOTE", body=full scope of examination response, title=examination response title). Expected content: a CRM engagement note that serves as the central record of the NCCOB examination response, covering the full scope: vendor payment controls (Premier Data Solutions), commission reconciliation (Marcus Webb), fee audit, inactive vendor billing (South Park Insurance Group), and the corrective action plan. Should reference the May 12 deadline.
OE 20: Post a message in the compliance-alerts channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id="C004", payload=general compliance response message). Expected content: a message in the compliance-alerts channel (C004) informing the core team that Keystone is in examination response mode. The message should be specific enough that the team knows to prioritize compliance work but general enough that company-wide specifics are not exposed, consistent with Denise's instruction to "keep it general enough that the rest of the company does not learn the specifics."

9 The compliance findings email body (visible in send_email or reply_to_email content parameter) sent to [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) identifies a root cause for the Premier Data Solutions exposure (e.g., single-signature approval gap for amounts under $1,000, no contract on file, no documentation supporting charges, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The compliance findings email identifies the PDS root cause as 'Single-signature vendor payment approval for amounts under $1,000 allowed recurring $600 invoices to be approved indefinitely without secondary review' and 'No vendor onboarding workflow requiring contract verification before first payment.'
✓Run #2The email states 'Root Cause: Single-signature approval threshold for amounts under $1,000 allowed recurring payments to bypass secondary review. No vendor onboarding or contract verification process enforced.'
✓Run #3The email identifies the root cause: 'Single-signature approval threshold of $1,000 allows indefinite recurring payments without secondary review. No vendor onboarding verification process exists.'
✓Run #4The email states 'Root Cause: Our vendor payment workflow requires only single-signature approval for amounts under $1,000' as the PDS root cause, and also notes 'No contract, statement of work, or purchase authorization on file'.
✓Run #5The email includes 'Root Cause: Single-signature approval for sub-$1,000 vendor payments with no cumulative threshold review. No mandatory vendor onboarding process requiring contract or SOW before first payment.' — clearly identifies root cause for PDS.
✓Run #6The compliance email identifies root cause as 'single-signature approval under $1,000' for Premier Data Solutions allowing indefinite recurring payments.
10 The compliance findings email body (visible in send_email or reply_to_email content parameter) sent to [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) identifies a root cause for the Marcus Webb commission leakage (e.g., LOS staff record still shows active with no termination date, Stripe payment channel never shut down, incomplete offboarding process, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The compliance findings email identifies the Webb root cause as 'no formal offboarding checklist exists,' 'LOS record was never updated to reflect termination,' and 'Stripe connected account was never deactivated.'
✓Run #2The email states 'Root Cause: No separation checklist linking HR/CRM actions to LOS status updates and payment rail deactivation. Commission payment process does not validate current employment status against LOS before disbursement.'
✓Run #3The email states: 'IT (Raj Anand) revoked LOS access but assumed finance handled Stripe. Finance (Hana) never received an offboarding notification. The LOS staff record — which drives automated commission workflows — was never updated with a termination date.'
✓Run #4The email identifies the Webb root cause: 'No offboarding checklist exists that ties staff termination to deactivation of payment rails... nobody disabled his Stripe connected account, updated his LOS status, or implemented a hold on commission payments'.
✓Run #5The email includes 'Root Cause: No offboarding protocol that requires (a) immediate LOS deactivation, (b) Stripe connected account disconnection, and (c) reconciliation of pending commission payments upon separation' — identifies root cause for Webb.
✓Run #6The compliance email identifies root causes for Webb as LOS record never updated to terminated and Stripe payment channel never deactivated during offboarding.
11 The compliance findings email body (visible in send_email or reply_to_email content parameter) sent to [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) includes corrective actions or remediation steps for the Premier Data Solutions exposure (e.g., suspend vendor payments, require dual-signature approval, obtain supporting documentation, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%

✓Run #3The email includes corrective actions: '(a) Immediately suspend all payments to Premier Data Solutions. (b) Implement dual-signature approval for ALL vendor payments regardless of amount. (c) Conduct full vendor master reconciliation against active contracts.'
✓Run #4The email lists corrective actions for PDS including: 'All payments to Premier Data Solutions suspended immediately', 'Implementing dual-signature approval for ALL vendor payments', and 'Vendor onboarding checklist being created requiring contract, W-9, physical address verification'.
✓Run #5Corrective actions for PDS include: 'All payments to Premier Data Solutions suspended immediately,' 'Implementing dual-signature approval for all vendor payments,' and 'Full vendor master reconciliation against active contracts.'

13 The compliance findings email body (visible in send_email or reply_to_email content parameter) sent to [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) identifies a root cause for the South Park Insurance Group billing gap (e.g., vendor deactivation process does not prevent new bills from being posted, no automated active-status check in the bill approval workflow, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The compliance findings email states the South Park root cause is 'No system-level control preventing bill creation for deactivated vendors' and 'No periodic audit of bills posted against inactive vendors.'
✓Run #2The email states 'Root Cause: QuickBooks does not enforce a hard block on bill creation for inactive vendors. No periodic reconciliation of vendor status against active billing.'
✓Run #3The email states: 'QuickBooks vendor deactivation does not automatically block new bill entry. No compensating control exists to flag bills posted to inactive vendors.'
✓Run #4The email states 'Root Cause: No system-level enforcement preventing bill entry against inactive vendors' for South Park Insurance Group.
✓Run #5Root cause for South Park: 'QuickBooks configuration does not enforce a hard block on posting bills to inactive vendors. No automated reconciliation between vendor status and bill posting.'
✓Run #6The compliance email and final response identify root cause for South Park as the system not enforcing a hard block on bill entry for deactivated vendors.
14 The compliance findings email body (visible in send_email or reply_to_email content parameter) sent to [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) includes corrective actions or remediation steps for the South Park Insurance Group billing gap (e.g., block new bills for inactive vendors, investigate and resolve the $702.86 bill, audit all inactive vendor accounts for similar issues, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The compliance findings email includes South Park corrective actions: 'Implement system controls or compensating manual review to block or flag bills against inactive vendors' and 'Conduct retroactive review of all inactive vendor bills.'
✓Run #2The email includes corrective actions for South Park: 'Determine whether South Park Insurance Group is still the legitimate E&O carrier,' and 'Implement quarterly vendor master audit comparing active billing to vendor status.'
✓Run #3The email includes corrective actions: '(a) Audit all bills posted to South Park Insurance Group after the vendor deactivation date... (d) Configure QuickBooks workflow rules to alert on bills submitted against inactive vendors.'
✓Run #4The email includes corrective actions for South Park: 'Implement QB workflow rule or manual review gate preventing new bills against inactive vendors', 'Quarterly vendor master audit comparing active/inactive status against recent bill activity', and steps to resolve the open payable.
✓Run #5Corrective actions for South Park include: 'Configure QuickBooks to enforce hard blocks on inactive vendor bill creation' and 'Reconcile all inactive vendors against recent bill activity.'
✓Run #6The comprehensive compliance email covered corrective actions for all three exposure areas including South Park inactive vendor controls.

35 The final response identifies the total Premier Data Solutions exposure as $6,000 (10 bills at $600 each over 10 months).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response clearly identifies 'Premier Data Solutions — $6,000.00 in unauthorized vendor payments' with '10 invoices at exactly $600/month'.
✓Run #2The final response states 'Premier Data Solutions — Suspected Fraudulent Vendor: 10 invoices × $600 = $6,000 total (July 2025–April 2026)'.
✓Run #3The final response states '$6,000 in suspected fraudulent invoices: 10 identical $600/month bills from July 2025–April 2026'.
✓Run #4The final response clearly states 'Premier Data Solutions -- $6,000.00 (Suspected Fraudulent Vendor)' and '10 invoices at exactly $600/month from July 2025 through April 2026'.
✓Run #5The final response states '10 identical $600 monthly invoices (July 2025 – April 2026)' and '2. Premier Data Solutions — Suspected Fraudulent Vendor: $6,000' — $6,000 total from 10 bills at $600 is clearly identified.
✓Run #6The final agent response explicitly states '$6,000.00 (10 invoices x $600)' for Premier Data Solutions.
36 The final response identifies that 2 of the 10 Premier Data Solutions bills were miscoded under Office Supplies instead of Data Services (October and November 2025).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response states '2 invoices miscoded to "Office Supplies" (the rest under "Data Services"), which is why the initial count was $4,800 — Hana confirmed the true total is $6,000.'
✓Run #2The final response states '2 bills miscoded under "Office Supplies" (the rest under "Data Services") — this is what Hana flagged to Victor on Slack, correcting the total from $4,800 to $6,000.'
✓Run #3The final response states 'Two bills were miscoded under "Office Supplies" (the rest under "Data Services"), which is why the initial count only showed $4,800 — Hana and Victor caught the discrepancy in Slack.'
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states 'Two months were miscoded under "Office Supplies" instead of "Data Services"' and identifies that Hana's initial count of 8 invoices missed these, with Victor finding the 2 miscoded ones.
✓Run #5The final response states '2 bills miscoded to Office Supplies (Oct/Nov 2025)' — directly identifies the 2 miscoded bills.
✓Run #6The final response states 'Two months were miscoded to Office Supplies which masked the true total (originally counted as $4,800; Hana confirmed corrected total of $6,000 in Slack after Victor flagged it)'.
37 The final response identifies the exact total of Marcus Webb's post-departure commission transfers as $7,655 (5 transfers).
outcome

51 The model must search QuickBooks bills using multiple queries or broad criteria when looking up Premier Data Solutions bills, rather than a single narrow search that would miss the 2 bills miscoded under Office Supplies (or must discover all 10 bills totaling $6,000).
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent performed a QuickBooks search with VendorRef.name LIKE '%Premier%' and fetchAll:true, which returned all 10 bills including the 2 miscoded under Office Supplies, successfully discovering the full $6,000 total rather than just the $4,800 from Data Services bills.
✓Run #2The agent ran multiple QuickBooks bill searches: first by VendorRef=143 (returned 0 results), then by VendorRef LIKE %Premier% (which returned all 10 bills). The Slack search also confirmed the $6,000 total including the 2 miscoded bills, indicating the agent found all 10 bills totaling $6,000.
✓Run #3The agent ran multiple QuickBooks bill searches: first with VendorRef='143' (which returned nothing), then with VendorRef LIKE 'Premier' (which found the bills). The email shows all 10 bills were found including the 2 miscoded under Office Supplies, demonstrating the multi-query approach successfully identified all $6,000.
✓Run #4The agent ran multiple QuickBooks bill searches: first with VendorRef=143, then with VendorRef LIKE 'Premier', and also searched Slack to discover the full context. The agent found all 10 bills totaling $6,000, including the 2 miscoded ones under Office Supplies.
✓Run #5The agent ran multiple QuickBooks searches: first by VendorRef ID (returned 0), then by VendorRef.name LIKE '%Premier%' (returned bills), and also used Slack message discovery to find the 2 miscoded bills. The agent correctly arrived at the $6,000 total by combining QB data with Slack confirmation from Hana, satisfying the multi-query requirement.
✓Run #6The agent ran multiple QuickBooks searches including by vendor ID and by amount ($600 flat fee), discovering all 10 bills totaling $6,000 including the 2 miscoded ones under Office Supplies.
